ops.text: Note-only blocks count as decision units in the focus deck. Presenting a board with informational markdown between decisions, the focus deck promotes each note-only run that ends at a section boundary or the end of the doc into its own indexed step: `focusSteps` flushes the pending run as a standalone `kind: 'context'` step (`web/src/focus.ts:50-101#m3nf`), and `FocusProgress` computes `total = steps.length` with no kind filter (`web/src/components/FocusProgress.tsx:10-50#sd95`). A deck with 2 approvals and 3 note runs reads "Step 1 / 5" and renders 5 tap dots, 3 of which can never fill (`stepStatus` returns null for a step with nothing to tally). The submit tally already knows notes aren't decisions — `BUILTIN_TALLIED` maps `markdown` to `null` (`web/src/decide.ts:20-50#weaq`) — but the step derivation doesn't share that filter. Notes should read as context attached to steps, not inflate the decision-unit count.
